body.title: CLOSED: CC.1 test minion — haiku-backed skill executor for end-to-end skill regression
body.result: CC1Minion(AgentBase): haiku executor + Bash interceptor; sprint_claim fixture; 5 unit tests + 1 live; commit 3194885a
body.description:
**Approved 2026-05-03 (Akien — 'this is a big lever').** Foundation for the capability-protocol family — closes the missing CC-side regression gap (Igor side is testable directly via pytest; CC behavior currently has no automated regression).

CC.1 is a haiku-backed Anthropic SDK process that loads a skill file, asks haiku to follow it, intercepts the resulting MCP/Bash calls, and asserts on the request shapes + vocabulary used (per theigors/rules/capability-protocol/vocabulary). Smoke-test fidelity, not certification — passing CC.1 is a strong signal that live-CC will follow the skill correctly; failing is a strong signal something is ambiguous.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/test_minions/cc1.py (NEW — haiku executor + tool-call interceptor), agent_datacenter/test_minions/__init__.py (NEW), agent_datacenter/test_minions/fixtures/ (NEW — skill test fixtures), tests/test_cc1_minion.py (NEW)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/capability-protocol (root), theigors/rules/inherit-base-class (CC.1 inherits AgentBase), theigors/rules/docs-live-in-code (cc1.py top-of-file docstring documents the executor contract).
**Scope boundary:** IN: haiku skill executor with intercepted tool calls, fixture format for skill tests, one example test exercising /sprint claim-step against a sandboxed cc_queue. OUT: full skill-test corpus (each downstream ticket adds its own fixtures). OUT: CC fidelity certification (CC.1 is smoke-test, not gold-standard).
**Test plan:** unit tests for the executor + interceptor (mocked Anthropic responses); one live-haiku integration test gated by ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var (skipped in CI without the key). Acceptance: cc1.run_skill('/sprint claim T-test-fixture-id') correctly identifies and emits the cc_queue.py claim call.
